name: Martin Winterkorn
bio: Martin Winterkorn served as the CEO of Volkswagen AG from 2007 until his resignation in September 2015 following the emissions scandal.
_evidence: As CEO during the onset of the scandal, he was the primary figure responsible for the company's initial denial and subsequent resignation, representing the failure of leadership that caused the crisis.

name: Matthias Müller
bio: Matthias Müller was the CEO of Volkswagen Group from 2015 to 2018, tasked with navigating the company through the post-Dieselgate recovery phase.
_evidence: He was the architect of the 'TOGETHER - Strategy 2025' plan, which pivoted the company toward electric vehicles and aggressive financial settlements to restore market position.

name: Herbert Diess
role: CEO of Volkswagen Brand (later Group CEO)
bio: Herbert Diess joined Volkswagen in 2015 to lead the VW brand and later became the CEO of the Volkswagen Group, focusing heavily on electrification.
_evidence: He aggressively pushed the company's massive transition to electric mobility as the primary strategy to move past the emissions scandal and rebuild consumer trust.

name: Gina McCarthy
role: Administrator of the U.S. Environmental Protection Agency (EPA)
bio: Gina McCarthy served as the Administrator of the EPA from 2013 to 2017, overseeing the agency's investigation into Volkswagen's emissions cheating.
_evidence: As the head of the EPA, she led the regulatory enforcement action that exposed the defeat device, forcing VW into the settlements that defined the recovery process.
